title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Creamy Butter Chicken Recipe | Murgh Makhani
seo.metaDescription: Master the best Butter Chicken recipe (Murgh Makhani) at home! This creamy, rich Indian classic is easier than you think. Get our authentic step-by-step guide for a restaurant-quality meal.
longTailKeywords: Butter Chicken recipe step by step
best Butter Chicken recipe at home
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy, rich Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro, ready to be served with naan.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of homemade Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) – a classic Indian dish perfect for any meal!

description: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian classic, famous for its rich, creamy tomato-based gravy and tender chicken pieces. This iconic dish offers a perfect balance of tangy, sweet, and mildly spiced flavors, making it a comforting and incredibly satisfying meal. It is a true crowd-pleaser, perfect for a special dinner or a delightful lunch.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, lemon juice, red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add 2 tablespoons of butter. Once melted, add the chopped onion, remaining 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Sauté until onions are soft and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the soaked cashews and tomato puree, mixing well.

instructions.title: Simmer and Blend the Gravy
instructions.text: Bring the gravy base to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-12 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the tomatoes to cook down. Remove from heat, discard the bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and cardamom pods (if desired, or leave for extra flavor). Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. You may need to add a splash of water if it is too thick.

instructions.title: Finish the Gravy
instructions.text: Return the smooth gravy to the pan. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter, sugar, the remaining 1/2 tsp garam masala, and crushed kasoori methi. Stir well and bring to a gentle simmer. Cook for another 3-5 minutes, allowing the flavors to deepen.

instructions.title: Combine and Serve
instructions.text: Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering gravy. Stir gently to coat the chicken. Pour in the heavy cream and mix until just combined. Do not boil after adding cream. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and a drizzle of extra heavy cream if desired. Serve hot with naan, roti, or basmati rice.

tips: For extra tender chicken, marinate overnight. The longer the marination, the deeper the flavor.
Ensure the gravy is blended super smooth for that classic restaurant-style texture. Use a fine-mesh sieve after blending if you prefer an even silkier consistency.
Adjust the sweetness and spiciness to your preference. A pinch more sugar can balance the tanginess, while extra red chili powder will increase the heat. Always taste and adjust before serving.
article:
Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani: Your Guide to a Legendary Indian Lunch

The aroma of Indian spices wafting from a simmering pot is an invitation to a culinary journey like no other. Among the myriad of exquisite dishes that define Indian cuisine, one stands out as a universally beloved masterpiece: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani. This iconic dish, famous for its rich, creamy, and subtly sweet-tangy tomato gravy enveloping succulent pieces of chicken, is a comfort food par excellence. Perfect for a hearty lunch, a special dinner, or any occasion that calls for something truly delicious, mastering this classic Butter Chicken Recipe is a rewarding endeavor for any home cook.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

Murgh Makhani, literally translating to "chicken with butter," is much more than its name suggests. It's a symphony of flavors and textures, a culinary hug in a bowl. Originating in Delhi, India, in the mid-20th century, Butter Chicken was born from the ingenious minds at Moti Mahal restaurant. They took leftover tandoori chicken, simmered it in a rich tomato-based gravy made luscious with butter and cream, and thus created a legend.

The hallmark of authentic Butter Chicken is its velvety smooth, vibrant orange-red gravy. This luxurious texture is achieved by carefully slow-cooking tomatoes, onions, cashews, and a blend of aromatic spices until tender, then blending and often straining the mixture to remove any fibrous bits. The chicken, typically marinated in yogurt and spices, is either grilled, pan-fried, or baked until tender before being introduced to the gravy, allowing it to absorb all the wonderful flavors. Unlike many spicier Indian curries, Murgh Makhani offers a milder, sweeter, and tangier profile, making it incredibly appealing to a wide range of palates. It’s the definitive Butter Chicken Recipe that defines comfort and indulgence.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting an unforgettable Murgh Makhani requires a thoughtful selection of fresh ingredients and a pantry stocked with essential Indian spices. Here’s what you’ll need to embark on this Butter Chicken Recipe adventure:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast (thighs are recommended for their tenderness and moisture)
*   Plain yogurt
*   Ginger-garlic paste
*   Red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder is ideal for color without excessive heat)
*   Turmeric powder
*   Garam masala
*   Salt
*   Lemon juice

For the Gravy Base:
*   Unsalted butter and a little oil (for sautéing)
*   Onions, finely chopped
*   Ginger-garlic paste
*   Canned crushed tomatoes or fresh ripe tomatoes, pureed
*   Cashews (soaked in hot water for 15-20 minutes, for creaminess)
*   Green cardamom pods, cloves, bay leaf (whole spices for tempering)

For the Gravy Spices & Finishing:
*   Cumin powder
*   Coriander powder
*   Red chili powder (again, Kashmiri for color)
*   Garam masala
*   Kasoori Methi (dried fenugreek leaves – absolutely crucial for authentic flavor)
*   Sugar (to balance the tang of tomatoes)
*   Salt, to taste
*   Heavy cream (for richness and smoothness)
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped (for garnish)

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Following this step-by-step Butter Chicken Recipe will guide you to a restaurant-quality meal:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** Cut the chicken into bite-sized pieces. In a bowl, combine the chicken with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, salt, and lemon juice. Mix well, cover, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours, or even overnight.

2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat a little oil in a pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook until lightly browned on all sides and cooked through. You can also grill or bake the chicken for a healthier option. Remove the cooked chicken and set aside.

3.  **Prepare the Gravy Base:** In the same pan (or a fresh pot), melt butter with a little oil. Add the whole spices (cardamom, cloves, bay leaf) and let them sizzle for a few seconds until fragrant. Add the chopped onions and sauté until softened and translucent. Stir in ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until the raw smell disappears.

4.  **Simmer and Soften:** Add the pureed or crushed tomatoes to the pot, along with the soaked cashews. Stir in cumin powder, coriander powder, and red chili powder. Cook this mixture on medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes break down, the mixture thickens, and the oil starts to separate from the sides (this could take 15-20 minutes).

5.  **Blend the Gravy:** Remove the whole spices (bay leaf, cardamom pods, cloves) if you wish, or leave them in for a stronger flavor. Let the mixture cool slightly, then transfer it to a blender. Blend until very smooth. For an extra silky gravy, pass the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pot, pressing down on the solids. Discard any remaining fibrous pulp.

6.  **Finish the Gravy:** Return the smooth gravy to low heat. Add the garam masala, kasoori methi (crush it between your palms before adding to release its aroma), sugar, and salt. Stir well and simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.

7.  **Combine and Serve:** Gently add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering gravy. Stir to coat the chicken thoroughly. Finally, stir in the heavy cream. Heat through for a couple of minutes, but avoid boiling vigorously after adding the cream, as it can curdle. Garnish generously with fresh cilantro.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Elevating your Butter Chicken Recipe to restaurant quality involves a few key techniques:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't rush the marination process. Longer marination times result in more tender, flavorful chicken.
*   **Use Chicken Thighs:** While breast meat works, chicken thighs remain juicier and more tender, even when slightly overcooked.
*   **Blend and Strain:** This is the secret to that incredibly smooth, velvety gravy. Don't skip the straining step if you want a truly refined texture.
*   **Kasoori Methi:** This dried fenugreek leaf is non-negotiable. Lightly toast it in a dry pan for a minute before crushing and adding to intensify its unique, slightly bitter, maple-like aroma.
*   **Balance Flavors:** Taste your gravy before adding the cream. Adjust sugar to counter the tomato's acidity and salt to your preference. Kashmiri chili powder provides beautiful color without excessive heat, allowing the other spices to shine.
*   **Good Quality Butter and Cream:** Since these are core ingredients, their quality significantly impacts the final taste and richness.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a complete meal in itself, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Indian accompaniments.

*   **Bread:** Warm, fluffy naan bread (garlic naan is a fantastic choice), tandoori roti, or flaky paratha are perfect for scooping up every last drop of that incredible gravy.
*   **Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a comforting base and soaks up the rich sauce beautifully.
*   **Sides:** A simple kachumber salad (diced cucumber, onion, tomato with a lemon dressing) or a cooling raita (yogurt dip) can offer a refreshing contrast to the richness of the curry.
*   **Garnish:** Always finish with a swirl of fresh cream and a sprinkle of chopped fresh cilantro for presentation and a burst of freshness.

## Variations and Substitutions

This versatile Butter Chicken Recipe can be adapted to suit various dietary needs and preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Alternatives:** For a meat-free version, replace chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or even a mix of vegetables like cauliflower, peas, and potatoes. Cook them separately before adding to the finished gravy.
*   **Healthier Options:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or substitute heavy cream with a lighter cream or even cashew cream for a dairy-free option. Grilling or baking the chicken instead of pan-frying also cuts down on fat.
*   **Spicier Kick:** If you prefer more heat, add a finely chopped green chili or increase the amount of red chili powder in the gravy.
*   **Nut-Free:** If you have a nut allergy, you can omit the cashews. To maintain some creaminess and body, you can use a small amount of cornstarch slurry (cornstarch mixed with water) added at the end, or rely on thoroughly cooked and blended onions and tomatoes for thickness.
*   **Dairy-Free:** Use a dairy-free yogurt for marinating the chicken. Substitute butter with a plant-based butter alternative or oil, and use full-fat coconut milk or coconut cream instead of heavy cream.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is more than just an Indian lunch recipe; it's an experience. Its creamy texture, balanced flavors, and comforting warmth make it a dish that satisfies the soul. Armed with this comprehensive Butter Chicken Recipe and these tips, you're now ready to recreate this iconic masterpiece in your own kitchen and delight your senses with every single bite. Enjoy the journey!
